meta.title: SDE Interview Revision Sheet
meta.description: Curated must-solve problems for SDE/SWE interviews. Each problem teaches a unique algorithm pattern.

problems.name: 2Sum Problem
problems.essence: Hash map for O(1) complement lookup — template for all pair-sum variants
problems.notes: Learn both hash map O(n) and two-pointer O(n log n) approaches

problems.name: Sort 0s 1s 2s (Dutch National Flag)
problems.essence: Three-pointer in-place partition — must know cold
problems.notes: lo/mid/hi pointers — can be done in one pass

problems.name: Majority Element (>n/2)
problems.essence: Boyer-Moore Voting — O(1) space majority detection
problems.notes: Cancel out pairs — the majority always survives

problems.name: Kadane's Algorithm (Max Subarray Sum)
problems.essence: Core 1D subarray DP — extend to max product, circular variants
problems.notes: Reset running sum when it goes negative

problems.name: Stock Buy and Sell I
problems.essence: One-pass min-so-far trick — baseline for all stock problems
problems.notes: Track min price seen so far, update max profit

problems.name: Next Permutation
problems.essence: Find decreasing suffix + rightmost bigger swap + reverse suffix
problems.notes: 3-step algorithm: find dip, find just-bigger, reverse tail

problems.name: Longest Consecutive Sequence
problems.essence: HashSet O(n) — only start counting from sequence heads
problems.notes: Only begin count when n-1 not in set (sequence head)

problems.name: Set Matrix Zeros
problems.essence: Use first row/col as flag storage — avoid extra space
problems.notes: Two-pass: mark flags, then apply zeros

problems.name: Rotate Image 90 Degrees
problems.essence: Transpose matrix then reverse each row
problems.notes: Transpose + horizontal reverse = 90° clockwise

problems.name: Spiral Matrix
problems.essence: Boundary shrinking with 4 pointers — directional simulation
problems.notes: top/bottom/left/right shrink after each layer

problems.name: Subarray Sum Equals K
problems.essence: Prefix sum + hash map — count all subarrays with sum K
problems.notes: prefixSum[i] - K seen before → valid subarray found

problems.name: 3Sum
problems.essence: Sort + two-pointer — skip duplicates carefully
problems.notes: Fix one, two-pointer rest — skip duplicates at all 3 positions

problems.name: Majority Element (n/3)
problems.essence: Extended Boyer-Moore for two candidates simultaneously
problems.notes: At most 2 elements can appear >n/3 times — track two candidates

problems.name: Merge Overlapping Intervals
problems.essence: Sort by start time + greedy merge
problems.notes: After sorting, merge when current.start <= last.end

problems.name: Count Inversions (Merge Sort)
problems.essence: Modified merge sort — count cross-inversions during merge
problems.notes: When left[i] > right[j], all remaining left elements also form inversions

problems.name: Reverse Pairs
problems.essence: Merge sort variant — count pairs where nums[i] > 2*nums[j]
problems.notes: Count in separate step before merge — 2x condition breaks merge logic if combined

problems.name: Maximum Product Subarray
problems.essence: Track both min and max — sign flips make min become max
problems.notes: Negative × negative = positive — always track both extremes

problems.name: Merge Two Sorted Arrays Without Extra Space
problems.essence: Gap method (Shell sort idea) or 3-pointer from ends
problems.notes: In-place O(1) space using gap = ceil((m+n)/2) shrinking each pass

problems.name: Pascal's Triangle
problems.essence: Row generation C(n,r) = C(n,r-1) * (n-r+1) / r — also 2D DP
problems.notes: Know both: generate row-by-row and find element using nCr formula

problems.name: Trapping Rain Water
problems.essence: Two-pointer with max-left/max-right — trapped = min(maxL, maxR) - height
problems.notes: FAANG favourite — also solvable with stack (monotonic)

problems.name: Product of Array Except Self
problems.essence: Left prefix products × right suffix products — no division allowed
problems.notes: Two passes: populate left products, then multiply right products on-the-fly

problems.name: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
problems.essence: Determine which half is sorted, then check if target is in that half
problems.notes: Always one half is sorted — binary check which one, then narrow

problems.name: Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array
problems.essence: Binary search to find the pivot / inflection point
problems.notes: If mid > right, pivot is in right half; else in left

problems.name: Find Peak Element
problems.essence: Binary search on unsorted — go towards the higher neighbor
problems.notes: Move to the side where arr[mid] < arr[mid+1] — peak exists there

problems.name: Single Element in a Sorted Array
problems.essence: Index parity trick — before single: even-indexed pair; after: odd
problems.notes: If mid is even and nums[mid]==nums[mid+1], single is to the right

problems.name: Koko Eating Bananas
problems.essence: Binary search on answer-space (speed) — check feasibility
problems.notes: Template: lo=1, hi=max(piles), check if feasible(mid) → narrow

problems.name: Median of 2 Sorted Arrays
problems.essence: Partition both arrays such that left halves combined = right halves
problems.notes: BS on smaller array — O(log min(m,n)); check max_left <= min_right

problems.name: Book Allocation / Split Array Largest Sum
problems.essence: Minimise the maximum — BS on answer, check if k partitions feasible
problems.notes: Same template as Koko — min possible = max element, max = total sum

problems.name: First & Last Position in Sorted Array
problems.essence: Two separate binary searches with different boundary conditions
problems.notes: Lower bound: go left when equal. Upper bound: go right when equal

problems.name: Find K Closest Elements
problems.essence: Binary search for left boundary of window of size k
problems.notes: Compare arr[mid] vs arr[mid+k] distance to x — shrink window accordingly

problems.name: Longest Palindromic Substring
problems.essence: Expand-around-center O(n) — handle odd and even centers
problems.notes: 2n-1 centers — also solvable with Manacher's O(n)

problems.name: Minimum Window Substring
problems.essence: Sliding window with frequency map — shrink when all chars covered
problems.notes: Expand right until valid, shrink left to minimise — track 'formed' count

problems.name: KMP / Find Index of First Occurrence
problems.essence: LPS (failure function) array — avoid redundant comparisons
problems.notes: LPS[i] = length of longest proper prefix which is also suffix

problems.name: Longest Common Prefix
problems.essence: Vertical scan char by char across all strings
problems.notes: Stop at mismatch or shortest string — also doable with trie

problems.name: Valid Anagram
problems.essence: Frequency array of 26 — increment for s, decrement for t
problems.notes: Base — extends to group-anagrams, find-all-anagrams problems

problems.name: Subarrays with K Different Integers (Atmost-K trick)
problems.essence: Exactly(k) = AtMost(k) - AtMost(k-1) — general window counting
problems.notes: Universal pattern for exact-count window problems

problems.name: Shortest Palindrome (KMP reverse trick)
problems.essence: Append reverse to original — KMP finds longest palindromic prefix
problems.notes: Concat s + '#' + rev(s), run KMP — last LPS value is the prefix length

problems.name: Group Anagrams
problems.essence: Hash sorted-string as key to group anagrams together
problems.notes: Key = sorted string or frequency tuple — O(n * k log k)

problems.name: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
problems.essence: Shrinking window — move left past the duplicate when found
problems.notes: Use last-seen index map to jump left pointer directly

problems.name: Reverse Linked List
problems.essence: 3-pointer iterative (prev, curr, next) — must be automatic
problems.notes: Know both iterative and recursive — sub-step in many LL problems

problems.name: Linked List Cycle II (Start of Loop)
problems.essence: Floyd's cycle detection + math: slow meets fast, then reset one to head
problems.notes: After meeting point, move one pointer to head — they meet at cycle start

problems.name: Merge K Sorted Lists
problems.essence: Min-heap of (value, node) — extract minimum, push next node
problems.notes: Alternative: divide and conquer merge — O(n log k)

problems.name: LRU Cache
problems.essence: DLL (for O(1) remove) + HashMap (for O(1) access) combined
problems.notes: Most recently used at tail, evict from head — key design interview question

problems.name: Add Two Numbers
problems.essence: Carry propagation on linked list nodes — build result list
problems.notes: Handle unequal lengths + remaining carry at end

problems.name: Palindrome Linked List
problems.essence: Find middle (slow/fast) + reverse second half + compare
problems.notes: In-place O(1) space — restore list after checking (good practice)

problems.name: Intersection of Two Linked Lists
problems.essence: Pointer switch trick: when A reaches end, redirect to B's head
problems.notes: Both pointers travel same total distance — meet at intersection

problems.name: Remove Nth Node From End
problems.essence: Two pointers with N-gap — when fast reaches end, slow is at target
problems.notes: Use dummy head to handle edge case of removing first node

problems.name: Reverse Nodes in K-Group
problems.essence: Recursively reverse groups of K, chain the results
problems.notes: Check if k nodes exist before reversing — don't reverse incomplete tail

problems.name: LFU Cache
problems.essence: Three maps: key→val, key→freq, freq→DLL of keys
problems.notes: Track min_freq for O(1) eviction — harder LRU variant

problems.name: Next Greater Element (Monotonic Stack)
problems.essence: Maintain decreasing stack — pop when current > top, assign NGE
problems.notes: Template for NGE, previous-greater, span — foundation of histogram problems

problems.name: Largest Rectangle in Histogram
problems.essence: Monotonic stack — find nearest smaller left and right for each bar
problems.notes: Area = height * (right_boundary - left_boundary - 1)

problems.name: Sliding Window Maximum (Deque)
problems.essence: Monotonic deque — maintain indices of useful elements only
problems.notes: Remove from front if out of window, from back if smaller than current

problems.name: Valid Parentheses
problems.essence: Stack — push open, pop and match on close
problems.notes: Foundation — extends to min-add, min-remove, score-of-parentheses

problems.name: Remove K Digits to Get Minimum Number
problems.essence: Greedy monotonic stack — remove larger digit when smaller arrives
problems.notes: Maintain increasing stack — use remaining budget if not yet used

problems.name: Stock Span Problem
problems.essence: Monotonic stack stores indices — span = current_idx - stack_top_idx
problems.notes: Accumulate span when popping — amortized O(1) per call

problems.name: Maximal Rectangle (in Binary Matrix)
problems.essence: Build histogram row by row + apply largest-rectangle on each
problems.notes: Heights[j] = 0 if matrix[i][j]==0, else heights[j]+1

problems.name: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
problems.essence: Shrink window when duplicate found — track last-seen index
problems.notes: Use char→last_index map to jump left pointer directly — avoids full shrink

problems.name: Max Consecutive Ones III (k flips allowed)
problems.essence: Shrink window when zero-count exceeds k
problems.notes: Count zeros in window — shrink from left when zeros > k

problems.name: Longest Repeating Character Replacement
problems.essence: Track dominant char count — replace others if (window - maxCount) <= k
problems.notes: maxCount never decreases — subtle but correct optimization

problems.name: Fruit Into Baskets / At Most 2 Distinct
problems.essence: At-most-k distinct chars window — shrink when exceeds k
problems.notes: Generalise to k — same template as Longest Substring At Most K Distinct

problems.name: Count Number of Nice Subarrays (exactly k odds)
problems.essence: Exactly(k) = AtMost(k) - AtMost(k-1) on integer arrays
problems.notes: Replace odd with 1, even with 0 — becomes binary subarray sum problem

problems.name: Maximum Points You Can Obtain from Cards
problems.essence: Total sum - min sum of middle subarray of size (n-k)
problems.notes: Reframe as: minimise the discarded middle portion

problems.name: Find Median from Data Stream
problems.essence: Two heaps: max-heap for lower half, min-heap for upper half
problems.notes: Median = top of max-heap (odd) or avg of both tops (even)

problems.name: Top K Frequent Elements
problems.essence: Min-heap of size K — maintains K largest frequencies
problems.pattern: Min Heap of Fixed Size K
problems.notes: Bucket sort alternative: index = frequency, O(n)

problems.name: Task Scheduler
problems.essence: Most frequent task controls idle time — greedy with max heap
problems.notes: Result = max(n * (maxFreq-1) + countOfMaxFreq, total_tasks)

problems.name: Kth Largest Element in a Stream
problems.essence: Fixed-size min-heap of K — root is always Kth largest
problems.notes: Add element, if size > k pop — root = kth largest

problems.name: Smallest Range Covering Elements from K Lists
problems.essence: Multi-list sliding window via min-heap — track current max separately
problems.notes: Push one from each list, track max, pop min and push next from same list

problems.name: Jump Game II (Minimum Jumps)
problems.essence: Track farthest reachable — update at each step boundary
problems.notes: Jump count increments when you pass current boundary

problems.name: Candy (Minimum Candies)
problems.essence: Two-pass greedy — left-to-right for right neighbors, right-to-left for left
problems.notes: Result[i] = max of left-pass[i] and right-pass[i]

problems.name: Non-overlapping Intervals (Min Removals)
problems.essence: Sort by end time — greedily keep intervals ending earliest
problems.notes: Classic activity selection problem — max non-overlapping = total - removed

problems.name: Job Sequencing Problem
problems.essence: Sort by profit descending — assign to latest available slot before deadline
problems.notes: Greedy: always take highest profit job that fits — DSU for fast slot finding

problems.name: Valid Parenthesis String (* wildcard)
problems.essence: Track range [lo, hi] of possible open counts — prune when lo < 0
problems.notes: * can be '(', ')' or '' — lo decrements, hi increments; valid if hi >= 0 at end

problems.name: Gas Station
problems.essence: If total gas >= total cost, solution exists — start where running sum < 0
problems.notes: Reset start point when cumulative tank < 0 — unique valid start exists

problems.name: Subsets / Power Set
problems.essence: Pick or don't pick — binary decision tree, 2^n subsets
problems.notes: Foundation of all backtracking — also via bitmask for compact solution

problems.name: Permutations
problems.essence: Swap-based recursion OR mark-visited + build array approaches
problems.notes: Know both swap-based and visited-array approach — handle duplicates variant too

problems.name: Combination Sum (unlimited picks)
problems.essence: Recurse with same index for unlimited pick — prune when sum > target
problems.notes: Combination Sum II: sort + skip duplicates at same level

problems.name: N-Queens
problems.essence: Row-by-row placement — check column + both diagonals with 3 bool arrays
problems.notes: col[], ldiag[], rdiag[] arrays for O(1) attack check

problems.name: Word Search (Grid Backtracking)
problems.essence: DFS + mark visited in-place — restore cell after recursion
problems.notes: Mark with '#', recurse in 4 directions, unmark on return

problems.name: Palindrome Partitioning
problems.essence: Precompute isPalin[i][j] DP + backtrack all cuts
problems.notes: Separate: generate all partitions (backtrack) vs min cuts (pure DP)

problems.name: Level Order Traversal (BFS)
problems.essence: Queue-based BFS — many variants: zigzag, right view, by level
problems.notes: Size-based inner loop gives level-by-level separation

problems.name: Height / Max Depth of Binary Tree
problems.essence: Recursive postorder — 1 + max(left height, right height)
problems.notes: Base of all tree height-based problems

problems.name: Diameter of Binary Tree
problems.essence: At each node: diameter candidate = leftHeight + rightHeight
problems.notes: Path doesn't have to pass through root — update global max at each node

problems.name: Binary Tree Maximum Path Sum
problems.essence: Gain = max(0, left_gain) + max(0, right_gain) + node.val at each node
problems.notes: Clamp negative subtree gains to 0 — return single-branch gain upward

problems.name: LCA of Binary Tree
problems.essence: Return node when found — if both children return non-null, current is LCA
problems.notes: Extends to graphs (binary lifting for O(log n) LCA)

problems.name: Binary Tree Right Side View
problems.essence: BFS: last node at each level OR DFS: root→right→left, first visit = right view
problems.notes: Know both BFS and DFS approaches

problems.name: Vertical Order Traversal
problems.essence: BFS with (node, col, row) — group by col, sort by row then val
problems.notes: Use map<col, map<row, multiset<val>>> for correct ordering

problems.name: Check Balanced Binary Tree
problems.essence: Return -1 on imbalance instead of true/false — single DFS pass
problems.notes: Naive O(n²) checks height repeatedly — O(n) propagates imbalance signal up

problems.name: Serialize and Deserialize Binary Tree
problems.essence: Preorder traversal with null markers — reconstruct from string
problems.notes: Use a queue/index to consume tokens during deserialization

problems.name: Construct Binary Tree from Preorder and Inorder
problems.essence: Preorder root splits inorder into left/right subtrees recursively
problems.notes: Use inorder index map for O(1) root location — reduces to O(n)

problems.name: Binary Tree Cameras (Min Cameras)
problems.essence: Greedy postorder — install camera at parent when child is uncovered
problems.notes: 3 states: 0=uncovered, 1=has camera, 2=covered — handle root specially

problems.name: Validate Binary Search Tree
problems.essence: Pass min/max bounds down recursively — every node must be in (min, max)
problems.notes: Common mistake: checking only direct children — bounds must propagate

problems.name: Kth Smallest Element in BST
problems.essence: Inorder traversal is sorted — count and return at k
problems.notes: Can also use augmented BST with subtree sizes for O(log n)

problems.name: Recover BST (Two Nodes Swapped)
problems.essence: Inorder — two violations indicate the swapped pair
problems.notes: First violation: first = prev, second = curr. Adjacent case: same node

problems.name: Convert Sorted Array to Balanced BST
problems.essence: Mid element as root — recursive on left and right halves
problems.notes: Guarantees height-balanced — inorder of result = original array

problems.name: BST Iterator (Controlled Inorder)
problems.essence: Simulate inorder with stack — push left spine on demand
problems.notes: O(h) space, O(1) amortised next — needed for merge-BSTs in-order

problems.name: Number of Islands
problems.essence: DFS/BFS flood fill — mark visited, count components
problems.notes: Template for all grid connected-component problems

problems.name: Rotten Oranges
problems.essence: Multi-source BFS — all rotten sources enqueued simultaneously
problems.notes: Level = time — count fresh oranges, decrement; if remaining > 0 return -1

problems.name: Course Schedule (Cycle Detection in DAG)
problems.essence: Kahn's BFS topo sort — cycle exists if not all nodes processed
problems.notes: If topoOrder.size() < n, cycle exists — Course Schedule II returns order

problems.name: Word Ladder (BFS on Implicit Graph)
problems.essence: BFS with character mutation — each word is a graph node
problems.notes: Try all 26 chars at each position — use set for O(1) word lookup

problems.name: Dijkstra's Algorithm
problems.essence: Priority queue (dist, node) — relax edges greedily, shortest path first
problems.notes: Fails with negative edges — use Bellman-Ford then

problems.name: Alien Dictionary (Topo Sort from Comparisons)
problems.essence: Build ordering graph from adjacent word pairs — topo sort gives alphabet order
problems.notes: Edge case: if word2 is prefix of word1, invalid — detect cycle = invalid input

problems.name: Accounts Merge (DSU on Strings)
problems.essence: Union-Find: union all emails in same account — group by root email
problems.notes: Map email → account name too — merge then reconstruct sorted lists

problems.name: Bipartite Graph Check
problems.essence: 2-coloring with BFS/DFS — conflict means not bipartite
problems.notes: Equivalent to cycle detection for odd-length cycles

problems.name: Cheapest Flights Within K Stops
problems.essence: Bellman-Ford for K iterations — or modified Dijkstra tracking stops
problems.notes: Relax edges K+1 times from source — use temp array to avoid over-relaxation

problems.name: Bridges in Graph (Tarjan's Algorithm)
problems.essence: DFS with disc[] and low[] — bridge when low[v] > disc[u]
problems.notes: low[v] = min(disc[v], min disc of back-edges reachable from v)

problems.name: Kosaraju's SCC Algorithm
problems.essence: Two DFS passes: fill finish order, then DFS on reversed graph
problems.notes: Each DFS on reversed graph in finish-order gives one SCC

problems.name: Pacific Atlantic Water Flow
problems.essence: Reverse BFS from both oceans — intersection is the answer
problems.notes: Mark reachable from Pacific and Atlantic separately — AND the two visited arrays

problems.name: House Robber / Max Non-Adjacent Sum
problems.essence: dp[i] = max(dp[i-1], dp[i-2] + nums[i]) — alternating choice
problems.notes: Space-optimise to two variables — House Robber II is circular variant

problems.name: Unique Paths (Grid DP)
problems.essence: dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] + dp[i][j-1] — base: first row/col = 1
problems.notes: With obstacles: dp[i][j] = 0 if obstacle; Unique Paths II variant

problems.name: 0/1 Knapsack
problems.essence: dp[i][w] = max(dp[i-1][w], dp[i-1][w-wt[i]] + val[i])
problems.notes: Template for all subset/partition DP — space optimise to 1D array

problems.name: Subset Sum Equals Target
problems.essence: Boolean knapsack — can we pick items summing exactly to target
problems.notes: Base for partition-equal-subset-sum, count-subsets-with-sum variants

problems.name: Coin Change (Minimum Coins)
problems.essence: Unbounded knapsack — dp[amount] = min over all coins of dp[amount-coin]+1
problems.notes: Init to infinity, dp[0]=0 — Coin Change II counts number of ways

problems.name: Longest Common Subsequence
problems.essence: dp[i][j] = 1+dp[i-1][j-1] if match, else max(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1])
problems.notes: Foundation for edit distance, shortest common supersequence, LCS variants

problems.name: Longest Increasing Subsequence
problems.essence: O(n log n) with patience sort / binary search on tails array
problems.notes: Know both O(n²) DP and O(n log n) binary search approaches

problems.name: Edit Distance
problems.essence: dp[i][j] = 1+min(insert, delete, replace) when chars differ
problems.notes: 3 operations — replace = dp[i-1][j-1]+1, insert = dp[i][j-1]+1, delete = dp[i-1][j]+1

problems.name: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock III (at most 2 txn)
problems.essence: State machine: 4 states — hold1, sold1, hold2, sold2
problems.notes: Template extends to k transactions (Stock IV) and cooldown/fee variants

problems.name: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock with Cooldown
problems.essence: 3 states: held, sold (cooldown), rest — transitions based on action
problems.notes: After selling, must rest — sold state feeds into rest state

problems.name: Matrix Chain Multiplication
problems.essence: Interval DP — dp[i][j] = min cost to multiply chain from i to j
problems.notes: Try all split points k — cost = dp[i][k] + dp[k+1][j] + dims product

problems.name: Burst Balloons
problems.essence: Think in reverse — which balloon to burst last in range [i,j]?
problems.notes: Add boundary balloons of value 1 — dp[i][j] = max coins in open interval (i,j)

problems.name: Palindrome Partitioning II (Min Cuts)
problems.essence: dp[i] = min cuts for s[0..i] — try all palindromic cuts ending at i
problems.notes: Precompute isPalin[i][j] in O(n²) — then dp is O(n²) total

problems.name: Distinct Subsequences
problems.essence: dp[i][j] = ways to form t[0..j-1] using s[0..i-1]
problems.notes: When chars match: dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j-1] + dp[i-1][j] (include or skip)

problems.name: Wildcard Matching
problems.essence: * matches zero or more chars — dp[i][j] depends on p[j]=='*'
problems.notes: If p[j]=='*': dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] || dp[i][j-1] — use or skip

problems.name: Largest Divisible Subset
problems.essence: LIS variant — condition is divisibility instead of strict increase
problems.notes: Sort array — if a|b and b|c then a|c, so chain property holds

problems.name: Regular Expression Matching
problems.essence: Harder than wildcard — 'a*' matches zero or more 'a's together
problems.notes: p[j]=='*': skip pair (dp[i][j-2]) or match one more (dp[i-1][j] if chars match)

problems.name: Implement Trie (Insert, Search, StartsWith)
problems.essence: 26-child node array — mark isEnd on insert, traverse on search
problems.notes: Foundation — extend to delete, count prefix matches, autocomplete

problems.name: Maximum XOR of Two Numbers in an Array
problems.essence: Bit trie — greedily pick opposite bit at each level for max XOR
problems.notes: Insert numbers bit by bit from MSB — query greedily for max XOR

problems.name: Number of Distinct Substrings
problems.essence: Insert all suffixes into trie — count nodes created (each = unique prefix/substr)
problems.notes: Each new edge = new distinct substring — count new node insertions

problems.name: Word Search II (Trie + Backtracking)
problems.essence: Build trie of all words — DFS on grid pruned by trie nodes
problems.notes: Prune dead trie branches during DFS — remove found words to avoid duplicates
